text:
Appart from light microscopes, scientists also use powerful electron microscopes that reveal the fine details of a cell structure. These instruments use a beam of electrons instead of light to produce highly magnified images, allowing us to see cell structure at the nanometre scale with remarkable clarity (a nanometre is one-billionth of a metre).

Estimate the size of the onion peel cell = 
Diameter of the visible field in micrometre

Suppose, 25 cells are seen along the diameter. In that case, the size of one onion cell would be 5000 µm/25 = 200 µm.

If the estimated size of an onion peel cell is 200 µm, how much does a light microscope magnify this cell? The total magnification of a microscope depends on its magnifying power of the eyepiece and the objective lens. If both the eyepiece and the objective lens have the magnifying power of 10X, then the total magnification will be 100X. This means that a cell with an estimated size of 200 µm will appear 100 times larger.

Thus, a microscope allows us to see very small structures clearly and is an essential tool for studying the cell structure. diagrams.label: Structure of a light microscope
diagrams.description: A side view diagram of a light microscope. The main components are labeled: The Base supports the entire structure. The Body tube connects the Eye piece (at the top right) and the Objective lens (located below the body tube). The Coarse adjustment knob is positioned above the body tube on the left. The Stage is the platform where the slide rests. Below the stage is the Fine adjustment knob. Underneath the stage, a Mirror is shown, reflecting light upwards.

diagrams.label: An electron microscope
diagrams.description: A side view diagram of an electron microscope. The structure rests on a Base. The Objective lens is positioned at the top, and the Stage is the platform located directly below the objective lens.
